name: g15_transport_shell_projection
purpose: Companion projection spec mapping the lifted G30 transport state into shell regions, anchors, and lens-readable positions.
projection_claim: The shell page should render a projection of the lifted G30 state machine, not invent an independent walk law.

region_model.statement: The stairs are the overlap region where lift/sign memory becomes legible in the shell.

frame_partition.statement: This draft partition gives the shell renderer a first canonical region assignment. It may be refined later, but should not be replaced by ad hoc arithmetic.

sheet_projection_rule.statement: The shell must treat sheet as inverse-face memory, not as a cosmetic flag.

orientation_classes.statement: Phase is preserved under full G15 and full G30 traversals in the current translator law, but remains part of the visible lifted state.

projection_rules.anchor_from_frame: Use frame as the primary anchor index unless an active lens declares a distinguished override.
projection_rules.inverse_anchor_from_sheet: If sheet='-', render the corresponding inverse-face counterpart of the anchor in the active lens.
projection_rules.cube_lens_override: In cube lens, v0 is always treated as the distinguished stairs midpoint / cube centroid anchor.
projection_rules.g15_shadow_rule: When rendering only the G15 shadow, sheet distinctions may be hidden but must not be contradicted.

first_full_walk.shell_meaning: same projected frame class, inverse-face counterpart reached, orientation not yet identity-restored

shell_page_must_not_assume: a pure G15 walk is the full law
double walking the G15 shadow is sufficient to reconstruct the lift
sheet can be discarded in the transport truth layer

anti_drift_rules: Do not let the page invent an arithmetic shell walk independent of the lifted state machine.
Do not collapse inverse-face transport into a color-only distinction.
Do not remove the distinction between projected anchor and inverse anchor.
Do not override the cube-lens stairs midpoint role of v0.
Do not treat provisional visual coordinates as the transport law itself.
